cells.text: Name of Company: DUB Therapeutics, Inc.
Title of Study: A Single and Multiple Ascending Dose Study to Evaluate Safety/Tolerability and Pharmacokinetics of Topical DUB-001 Eyedrops in Patients with Persistent Corneal Epithelial Defect
Study Center(s): TBD [TBD] [City, State, Country, Zip Code]

This study will be conducted in 2 parts. Part 1 is a single ascending dose study that will be conducted in a single center with healthy volunteers on a Phase 1 unit. Part 2 is a multiple ascending dose study that will be conducted in patients with mild to moderate persistent corneal epithelial defect (PCED) at multiple sites in US, EU and AUS. OBJECTIVES: Part 1 Primary: The primary objective of this study is to compare the safety and tolerability of placebo aqueous eye drops to a single drop of DUB-001 at ascending doses in healthy adult volunteers and to determine the recommended phase 2 dose (RP2D). Secondary: The secondary objective is to characterize the pharmacokinetic (PK) profile of DUB-001 in tears and plasma. Part 2 Primary: The primary objective of this study is to compare the preliminary efficacy of placebo aqueous eye drops to multiple doses of the RP2D of DUB-001 administered at various frequencies. Secondary: The secondary objective is to characterize the safety, tolerability and pharmacokinetic (PK) profile of DUB-001 in plasma.

Part 1 Number of Healthy Volunteers: Approximately 27 male and female volunteers (3 cohorts of 9 volunteers each) will be enrolled in the study at 1 investigational site Part 2 Number of Patients: Approximately 36 male and female patients (3 cohorts of 12 patients each) will be enrolled in the study at up to 30 investigational sites

cells.text: Abbreviations BCVA = best corrected visual acuity; Con = concomitant; DLT = Dose-Limiting Toxicity; ECG = electrocardiogram; ETDRS = early treatment diabetic retinopathy study; HIPPA= Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act; IOP = intraocular pressure; PK = pharmacokinetics; TEAE = treatment emergent adverse event; gtt=drop ; OU = Both eyes 1Washout: Washout of topical medications that may confound the results of treatment with DUB-001. 2Vital signs include temperature (oral, tympanic, or temporal), resting heart rate, and blood pressure. 3Demographics include age, gender, race, ethnicity, height, and weight. 4Ocular medical history shall include but is not limited to shall include but is not limited to lesion characteristics, eye involvement, and localization (central versus non-central) Brief physical examination includes appearance, mood, and affect only. Physical examination for symptom driven findings may be conducted at any time during the study. 5Brief physical examination includes appearance, mood, and affect only. Physical examination for symptom driven findings may be conducted at any time during the study. 6Urine/Serum pregnancy tests shall be performed in females of childbearing potential only. If the test is positive, then a confirmatory serum pregnancy test must be conducted. 7Serum Sampling PK in Part 1 and 2-- blood will be collected from participants at hours TBD after instillation for PK 8Tear sampling PK in Part 1 and 2--tears will be collected from participants at hours TBD after instillation. 9Symptoms: The severity of ocular symptoms (burning/stinging, itching, foreign body, discomfort, dryness, photophobia, and pain) shall be evaluated on a visual analog scale of 0 to10. Will be evaluated pre and post dose. 10Selection of Study Eye: Subjects with persistent epithelial defect of the cornea. If both eyes (OU) are equally affected, then the nondominant eye will be treated 11Rescue includes patch therapy or medical therapy using approved medications and according to the physician’s discretion

• BCVA with current correction (the power of the present correction recorded) at distance and, when appropriate, at near. • BCVA will be recorded as total letter score in each eye following refraction. Visual acuity testing should precede any examination requiring contact with the eye. • In order to provide standardization and well-controlled assessments of BCVA during the study, all BCVA assessments must be performed by trained staff who are certified on the study procedure using certified VA lanes.
ETDRS (number of letters correctly identified) and LogMAR OS and OD

• Iris pigmentation to be determined grossly as: Black, Blue, Brown, Hazel, Green, Grey, Other (to be specified); and indicated as a number 1-24 (see Appendix 22.8) • Eyelid position and character, lashes, lacrimal apparatus and tear function; globe position; and pertinent facial features • Ocular signs and symptoms of irritation as indicated by redness (see Appendix 22.9)
Black, Blue, Brown, Hazel, Green, Grey, Other and Color 1-24 Normal Abnormal (CS, not CS)
• Size and response to light, relative afferent pupillary defect

• Corneal sensitivity corneal sensitivity using easy-to- use, low-cost materials that are readily accessible to eye care providers (either a cotton swab, a piece of dental floss, or a finely tapered tissue)..

• Eyelid margins and lashes; tear film; conjunctiva; sclera; cornea; anterior chamber (AC); • Presence of cells or flare (see Appendix 22.13) and assessment of central and peripheral anterior chamber depth; • Iris, lens, and anterior vitreous, prior to dilation. • Perform fluorescein instillation and examination of cornea with blue light.
Normal Abnormal (CS, not CS; Grading of AC cells and flare scale of 0- 4) (see grading chart below)

• Preferably with a contact applanation method (typically a Goldmann tonometer) if it can be tolerated • Baseline IOP: The baseline IOP is defined as the pressure obtained on Day –1, at screening and prior to onset of Treatment. Each IOP is reported as the mean of three (3) measures. • Calibration of Tonometers • Per the Sponsor, a calibration certificate dated not more than 30 days prior to the first volunteer enrolled shall be presented.
Mean IOP, of 3 measures, OS and OD
• Mid and posterior vitreous, retina (including posterior pole and periphery), vasculature, and evaluation of optic nerve using mydriatic drops and accessory diagnostic lenses

• Anterior Segment OCT-High definition images of the corneal anatomy (corneal epithelium, stroma, and endothelium), using anterior segment cube 512 x 128 as performed on Cirrus 4.0 HD-OCT or equivalent • Pachymetry map as generated using Cirrus 4.0 HD- OCT or Pentacam • Central corneal thickness as generated using Cirrus 4.0 HD-OCT or equivalent (Appendix 22.11)
Normal Abnormal (CS, not CS) In study eye Thickness from 350-700 microns
• Densitometric map of corneal scars as generated using Pentacam

cells.text: Severe with symptoms (photo B or C) with stinging, burning, blurred or dim vision
The volunteer starts are the top of the chart and begins to read down the chart. The volunteer
reads down the chart until he or she reaches a row where a minimum of three letters on a line
cannot be read. The volunteer is scored by how many letters could be correctly identified.
volunteers will be evaluated after dilation with anterior segment OCT for cornea anatomy
corneal epithelium, stroma, and endothelium; Figure 5
anatomy as normal or abnormal. If the anatomy is determined to be abnormal, the investigator
will determine whether the abnormality is clinically significant or nonsignificant. Any clinically
significant change after dosing will be recorded on the AE page of the eCRF.
